params.name: Geneva Declaration
params.description:
On Oct 21, 1948 AD, the Genava Declaration was officially adopted by the World Medical Association.
The Geneva Declaration is an oath that physicians must read before practicing. Based on the Hippocratic Oath, it specifies the necessary medical ethics, ideological qualities, and behavioral requirements for physicians, constituting a code of ethics for the medical profession.
